seoTitle: How to Send GIFs in Slack Using the Built-In Picker
metaDescription: Send animated GIFs in Slack using the native GIF picker or the GIPHY slash command, and manage content ratings for your team.
intro: Dropping a quick animated reaction into a Slack chat is easy once you know where the GIF tools live. This guide covers the native built-in picker as well as the classic GIPHY slash command for sharing animations.

steps.heading: Open the emoji menu
steps.body: Click the smiley face icon in the message input field, then switch to the native GIFs tab.

steps.heading: Search and insert a GIF
steps.body: Type a mood keyword into the search bar and click an animation to instantly insert it into your message.

steps.heading: Install GIPHY for slash commands
steps.body: Go to Apps and install the official GIPHY package to unlock the classic command-line sharing option.

steps.heading: Use the /giphy command
steps.body: Type /giphy followed by a search term inside the message field to generate a preview card.

steps.heading: Shuffle and send
steps.body: Click Shuffle to cycle through alternative results, then click Send to post the final GIF to the channel.

faq.question: Can I control what content ratings appear in GIF searches?
faq.answer: Yes, workspace admins can set content maturity filters to keep GIF results appropriate for the team.

faq.question: Does the native GIF picker require any installation?
faq.answer: No, the native picker is built into Slack and doesn't require installing any additional app.

faq.question: Why do I only see a preview before posting with /giphy?
faq.answer: The ephemeral preview lets you confirm or shuffle the result before it's visible to the rest of the channel.

faq.question: Can I send GIFs in direct messages too?
faq.answer: Yes, both the native picker and the /giphy command work the same way in direct messages.
